_meta.name: STING v6 service corridor definitions
reference_docs: CIBSE Guide B: Heating, ventilating, air conditioning and refrigeration
notes: Band definitions are suspended-ceiling plenum layouts. Below-slab
and raised-floor service corridors are handled by a separate

bands.label: Hot water service
bands.notes: Kept high to minimise heat loss via ambient air circulation.

bands.label: Gas supply
bands.notes: Highest of all services per BS 6891 gas rising.

bands.label: Low-temp hot water heating
bands.notes: Can share CWS band if flow direction and insulation agree.

bands.label: Foul + sanitary drainage
bands.notes: Lowest of all services — gradient required, per BS EN 12056.

bands.label: Power cable tray
bands.notes: Above all services to reduce induction risk per BS 7671.

bands.label: Data / telecoms cable tray
bands.notes: Stacked above power per BS EN 50174-2; min 200 mm separation checked by S4.8.

bands.label: Security / fire low-voltage
bands.notes: Partially overlaps data band — fire cables must be separate per BS 5839-1.

bands.label: Fire sprinkler supply
bands.notes: Highest band reserved for wet sprinkler supply.

bands.label: HVAC supply air ductwork
bands.notes: Ducts are large — band is tall. Surround with CIBSE 150 mm clearance.
